reasoning: The Lean statement correctly formalizes the informal claim. The list `as : List G` represents the sequence $a_1, a_2, \dots, a_n$; `as.prod⁻¹` is $(a_1a_2\cdots a_n)^{-1}$; and `(as.reverse.map (λ x => x⁻¹)).prod` reverses the list to get $[a_n, a_{n-1}, \dots, a_1]$, inverts each element to get $[a_n^{-1}, a_{n-1}^{-1}, \dots, a_1^{-1}]$, and takes the product, yielding $a_n^{-1}a_{n-1}^{-1}\cdots a_1^{-1}$. This matches the informal RHS exactly. The universal quantification over any group `G` and any list matches "for all $a_1,\dots,a_n\in G$". This encoding is essentially Mathlib's `List.prod_inv_reverse` (up to the interchangeable `map_reverse` identity). No hypotheses are added or dropped.
